title: Sub-lemma: $W$ genuinely lies in $\mathcal W(\Pi,\psi^{-1})$ and the off-mirabolic translation identities

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
**Claim needed.** Two bookkeeping-but-load-bearing facts:

1. **Realizability in the Whittaker model.** The test datum used (often specified by its values on the mirabolic / in the Kirillov model) must arise from a genuine $W\in\mathcal W(\Pi,\psi^{-1})$ on all of $\mathrm{GL}_{n+1}(F)$, not merely a function on the mirabolic $P_{n+1}$. Justify via the Bernstein–Zelevinsky filtration / density of mirabolic restrictions and the injectivity of restriction to $P_{n+1}$.

2. **Translation identity.** The identities relating $W(\operatorname{diag}(g,1)u_Q)$ to mirabolic Whittaker values — e.g. the off-mirabolic right-translation identity of the form
$$W(\operatorname{diag}(g,1)\,t_Q)=\psi(-g_{nn})\,W(\operatorname{diag}(g,1)\,d_Q^{-1})$$
(right-translating by the relevant element of $N_{n+1}$) — must be **proved**, using the left $N_{n+1}$-equivariance $W(ux)=\psi^{-1}(u)W(x)$ and the explicit form of $u_Q=I_{n+1}+QE_{n,n+1}$. In a previous attempt these were asserted without proof.

**To prove.** State precisely which translates of $W$ appear and verify each identity from the Whittaker equivariance and the matrix algebra of $u_Q$, $\operatorname{diag}(g,1)$.
